overview: Naya Ramp accelerates mana production to deploy threats ahead of schedule and maintain board presence. The deck leverages landfall synergies and card draw engines to out-resource opponents over time. By stabilizing early with efficient creatures, it transitions into a late-game flood of high-impact permanents.
win_conditions: Mana advantage enabling multiple spells per turn
Card advantage through landfall and draw engines
Overwhelming board presence with large, efficient creatures

keep_criteria: Two to three lands in hand
At least one early ramp spell or low-cost threat
Mana base coverage for W/R/G spells
Keep hands with 2-3 lands and at least one ramp spell (e.g., Skyshroud Claim, Farseek, or a mana creature) or a turn-2 land drop.
Prioritize hands with Utopia Sprawl or a 3-mana threat if paired with ramp.
mulligan_criteria: All cards cost four or more mana
Mulligan pure land hands, hands with only 1-2 lands, or hands with multiple threats but zero ramp or fixing.

early_game: Prioritize land drops and ramp spells
Play efficient threats to apply early pressure
Turn 1: Play a land, cast a ramp spell or play a mana creature.
Turn 2: Play a land, cast another ramp or a 2-mana threat.
Turn 3: Drop Utopia Sprawl if you have the mana, or cast a 3-mana threat like Generous Ent.
Sequence lands to ensure you hit 4-5 mana by turn 3-4.
Chain multiple ramp spells to hit 5 mana by turn 3 to drop Avenging Hunter or multiple threats.
mid_game: Trigger landfall synergies with Utopia Sprawl
Deploy Generous Ent and Avenging Hunter for card advantage
Maintain board presence while filtering deck
Prioritize mana acceleration over card advantage early.
save Utopia Sprawl for your biggest threats.
Manage hand size by casting threats when you can't improve the board further.
Avoid flooding by converting excess ramp into card selection or threats once you hit 5 mana.
Commit threats when you have protection or can trade favorably.
late_game: Flood the board with large creatures
Use excess mana to play multiple threats per turn
Close out games with overwhelming stats and card draw
Avenging Hunter and Generous Ent with Utopia Sprawl are primary finishers.
Once you have a 6/6 or 7/7 with haste, push for lethal.
Use additional threats to overwhelm blockers or trade into sweepers.
Utopia Sprawl can also be cast on a cheaper threat to win combat or push damage.
Go all-in when you have Utopia Sprawl on board, a clear path to the opponent, and no immediate sweepers in play.
Also commit when you can trade threats favorably or when the opponent is at low life.

key_cards.name: Avenging Hunter
key_cards.role: Early-game threat and card draw engine
key_cards.notes: Play on land drops to trigger its draw ability. Use as a clock to pressure aggressive opponents while refilling your hand.

key_cards.name: Generous Ent
key_cards.notes: Deploy to tap for extra mana and draw cards. Prioritize playing it when you have open mana to maximize its utility.

key_cards.name: Utopia Sprawl
key_cards.notes: Triggers on every land entering the battlefield. Use it to draw cards and generate mana, but be mindful of its upkeep cost.

sequencing_tips: Play lands before ramp spells to maximize landfall triggers
Hold up mana for counters or removal when facing interactive decks
Deploy threats before ramp when ahead to pressure opponents
Prioritize card draw engines when behind to catch up
Common mistake: casting Utopia Sprawl on a 2-drop instead of a 4/5 threat.
Another: over-ramping into a dead hand.
Remember that Utopia Sprawl is an enchantment, so it can be destroyed by artifact/enchantment hate.
Sequence lands carefully to avoid flooding.
In the mirror, card advantage and efficient removal win games.
Always track their life total and board state before committing to a Utopia Sprawl play.

matchups.advice: Play early threats and use removal efficiently. Prioritize survival until your ramp engines come online.
Ramp past early interaction and flood the board with threats. Use card draw to find answers to their counterspells.
Race with efficient threats and leverage card advantage. Avoid trading up into their larger creatures.

Use multiple threats in a row to force counterspells. Cast Utopia Sprawl on your most expensive threat to make it harder to counter or trade. Use cheap threats to bait counters. If they have heavy counter magic, pivot to enchantment-based strategies or sideboard in protection.

Race them with faster threats. Use Utopia Sprawl to win combat and stabilize. Bring in artifact/enchantment hate if they rely on it. Don't overcommit to big threats; trade favorably and pressure their life total.

Win by out-valuing. Use card draw, removal, and better sequencing. Utopia Sprawl on a cheaper threat can win trades. Track their hand to know when they have answers. Prioritize board presence and force them to use removal inefficiently.

common_threats: Watch for sweepers, targeted removal, and counterspells.
Note if the opponent is playing discard or hand disruption.
Track their mana development to predict when they can answer your threats.
Pay attention to graveyard activity if they play reanimation or recursion.
